senses.definition: used to refer to a person or thing that is completely motionless

examples.x: As the meditation session progressed, she became as still as a stone, finding inner peace and calm.
The lake was so calm and serene that its surface appeared as still as a stone, reflecting the surrounding landscape.
In the face of adversity, he remained as still as a stone, maintaining his composure and not showing any signs of distress.
The statue stood in the park, as still as a stone, capturing the attention of passersby with its lifelike appearance.
The child was so engrossed in the bedtime story that he lay in bed, as still as a stone, listening intently to every word.

senses.definition: used to refer to someone or something that is very difficult to lift or move, due to being heavy

examples.x: Carrying the suitcase up the stairs felt like a daunting task as it was as heavy as lead.
The silence in the room was heavy as lead, creating an atmosphere of tension and unease.
After the intense workout, her legs felt as heavy as lead, making each step a struggle.
The burden of guilt weighed on his conscience, making it feel as heavy as lead.
The backpack, filled with books and supplies, was heavy as lead, causing strain on her shoulders.

senses.definition: used to refer to someone or something that is very easy to lift and carry, due to having no considerable weight

examples.x: With the new aerodynamic design, the racing car felt light as a feather on the track.
She danced across the stage, feeling as light as a feather, gracefully moving with each step.
The helium-filled balloon soared in the sky, floating light as a feather above the crowd.
As I let go of all my worries, I felt my mind becoming light as a feather, finding a sense of tranquility.

senses.definition: (of a thing) in its most natural or true state of existence

examples.x: The artist preferred to paint landscapes in the raw, capturing the beauty of nature untouched by human intervention.
"The chef presented the dish in the raw, highlighting the freshness and natural flavors of the ingredients.
The photographs were exhibited in the raw, without any editing or post-processing, showcasing the raw emotions captured in the moment.
The musician performed the song in the raw, using only an acoustic guitar and her voice, creating an intimate and raw musical experience.

senses.definition: a person or thing that is very strange or unattractive in appearance

examples.x: The new sculpture in the park was certainly a sight for sore eyes, with its bizarre and unconventional design.
After being stuck in a concrete jungle for weeks, seeing the vast landfill was a sight for sore eyes, albeit an unattractive one.
The old abandoned house was falling apart and covered in graffiti, truly a sight for sore eyes in the otherwise picturesque neighborhood.
The fashion disaster walking down the street in mismatched colors and clashing patterns was a sight for sore eyes.
The actor's exaggerated makeup and over-the-top costume on stage were definitely a sight for sore eyes, distracting from the performance.

senses.definition: used to say that something is extreme or exciting

examples.x: The new roller coaster at the amusement park is off the rails!
The concert last night was off the rails!

senses.definition: the devil or any character that has similarities to it

examples.x: She made a deal with Old Harry, and now she's paying the price for her reckless actions.
The old abandoned church was rumored to be a gathering place for worshippers of Old Harry.
He was warned not to dabble in the occult, as summoning Old Harry could have dire consequences.
Legend has it that Old Harry roams the countryside at night, seeking souls to torment.
In the dark corners of the forest, locals whispered of encounters with Old Harry, a sinister presence lurking in the shadows.

senses.definition: mail that is delivered physically by the postal system as opposed to email and other electronic means

examples.x: I sent my application for the job via snail mail, but I'm worried it won't arrive on time.
In the age of instant messaging, snail mail seems like a relic of the past.
I prefer to receive my bills via email, as I find snail mail to be too slow and inconvenient.
I sent a birthday card to my grandmother in snail mail, as I know she enjoys receiving handwritten letters.
If you need to send something urgently, snail mail is not the best option.

senses.definition: a person's fist

examples.x: The enraged boxer aimed a bunch of fives at his opponent's jaw.
The bully threatened to give the scrawny kid a bunch of fives if he didn't hand over his lunch money.

senses.definition: referring to something that is stolen or not in its usual place

examples.x: The valuable painting was mysteriously AWOL from the museum's collection.
The diligent student's homework assignments were consistently AWOL, leading to concerns about their academic performance.

senses.definition: to find a thing that one was searching for

examples.x: I finally got a hold of the book you recommended, and I can't wait to start reading it.
Can you help me get a hold of the contact information for the conference organizers?
It took a while, but I managed to get a hold of the rare vinyl record I've been searching for.
I need to get a hold of my passport before the trip; otherwise, I won't be able to board the flight.
After hours of searching, I was relieved to finally get a hold of the keys I thought I had lost.

word_list.pronunciation: lˈeɪ ɡɛt wˈʌnz hˈændz ˌɑːn ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to succeed in obtaining something

examples.x: I finally laid my hands on the antique vase I've been searching for at the flea market.
It took a bit of effort, but I managed to get my hands on a copy of the rare collector's edition.
Can you help me get my hands on the report that was filed last week?
The detective was determined to lay his hands on the evidence that would crack the case wide open.
After months of searching, I was thrilled to finally get my hands on the perfect wedding dress.

senses.definition: ready to be used or obtained

examples.x: The last few tickets for the concert are up for grabs, so make sure to buy them quickly.
The job position is up for grabs, and anyone meeting the qualifications can apply.
The company announced a surprise bonus, and employees are excited to see what's up for grabs.
With the store-wide sale, many items are up for grabs at significantly discounted prices.
The leadership role in the community project is up for grabs, and interested individuals can express their interest.

senses.definition: to be available for use only due to being unwanted

examples.x: The extra office supplies will go spare if no one claims them by the end of the week.
The unsold tickets for the event will go spare, so they are now available at a discounted rate.
After the renovation, some furniture went spare and is now offered to employees for their home offices.
The company had ordered too much inventory, and the excess products are going spare at a clearance sale.
If no one in the office wants the spare computer equipment, it will go spare and be donated to a local charity.

senses.definition: (of an object) clear or thin enough for one to be able to see through it
adhering to traditional values, methods, or styles

examples.x: His taste in music is decidedly old school, with a love for classic rock and jazz.
The professor's teaching style is old school, relying on in-depth lectures and handwritten notes.

senses.definition: extremely or unusually small in size or importance

examples.x: Compared to the grandeur of the neighboring buildings, the quaint little café on the corner is knee-high to a grasshopper.
The budget for our project is knee-high to a grasshopper, making it challenging to achieve our ambitious goals.
When I first started working at the company, my opinion was considered knee-high to a grasshopper.

senses.definition: very appealing to the eyes

examples.x: Francesca was as pretty as a picture and apparently glowing with health.
This charming cottage dates back to the 15th century and is as pretty as a picture, with its thatched roof and secluded garden.

senses.definition: used to refer to a place that is completely silent or quiet, with no noise or sound at all

examples.x: The room fell silent as the grave when the teacher walked in, signaling the start of the exam.
In the aftermath of the intense storm, the neighborhood was as silent as the grave, with no electricity or sounds of activity.
As the detective entered the crime scene, the air became as silent as the grave, heightening the tension in the room.
The library was silent as the grave, with patrons engrossed in their reading and study.
When the power went out during the night, the house became as silent as the grave, creating an eerie atmosphere.

senses.definition: used of someone or something that is seen or considered very normal in America
used to say that something is not following the planned or expected course

examples.x: The politician's campaign was expected to be a smooth ride, but it's been plagued by scandals and is now off the rails.

senses.definition: in alignment with a specific path, course, or set of guidelines within a given context or framework

examples.x: The team is working along the line of the project plan, ensuring tasks are completed on schedule.
They are developing new products along the line of customer feedback and market demands.
The company operated along the line of sustainable practices, reducing its environmental impact.
The artist created remarkable works of art along the line of abstract expressionism, pushing artistic boundaries.
The organization will continue to innovate along the line of technological advancements, staying ahead of the competition.
